differential_diagnoses: Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
next_steps: Perform comprehensive transthoracic echocardiography to assess current valve gradients and ejection fraction
Laboratory testing including CBC, iron studies, BNP, and creatinine
Cardiac catheterization or coronary CT angiography to assess for obstructive coronary artery disease
Referral for heart team evaluation for potential transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR)
Fecal immunochemical test or colonoscopy to evaluate for occult gastrointestinal blood loss
Consider 24-hour Holter monitoring to evaluate for occult arrhythmia

differential_diagnoses: Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
Chronotropic incompetence due to beta-blocker therapy
Pulmonary disease (COPD or interstitial lung disease)
next_steps: Perform urgent transthoracic echocardiography to assess valvular gradients and diastolic function
Obtain laboratory studies including CBC, ferritin, iron studies, NT-proBNP, creatinine, and TSH
Perform electrocardiogram to evaluate for LVH, conduction delays, or ischemia
Evaluate for occult blood loss with fecal immunochemical test
Consult cardiology Heart Team for TAVR evaluation
Obtain coronary CT angiography for pre-procedural planning
